---
title: Better science communication with<br><quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> apps
subtitle: <em>With <rosa>remote sensing</rosa> examples</em> | <gris>2026-04-01</gris>
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#224289"
author:
  - name: <vhg>Víctor Gauto, **MS** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-9960-8558)</vhg>
    affiliation:
      - name: <rosa><b>GISTAQ</b> (UTN-FRRe)</rosa>
        url: https://www.instagram.com/gistaq.utn/
      - name: <rosa><b>IIDTHH</b> (UNNE, CONICET)</rosa>
        url: https://iidthh.conicet.gov.ar/
      - name: <rosa><b>Instituto Gulich</b> (UNC, CONAE)<rosa>
        url: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/
    email: victor.gauto@ca.free.utn.edu.ar
    corresponding: true
  - name: Matías<br>Bonansea, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1953-2595)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>ICBIA</b> (UNRC, CONICET)
        url: https://icbia.conicet.gov.ar/
  - name: Anabella<br>Ferral, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-9383-7728)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>Instituto Gulich</b> (UNC, CONAE)
        url: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/
  - name: Osvaldo<br>Cardozo, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0345-4505)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>IIDTHH</b> (UNNE, CONICET)
        url: https://iidthh.conicet.gov.ar/
  - name: Claudia<br>Giardino, **PhD** [{{< iconify fa6-brands:orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-3937-4988)
    affiliation:
      - name: <b>IREA</b> (CNR)
        url: http://www.irea.cnr.it/
format:
  revealjs:
    fig-width: 7
    fig-height: 7
    fig-dpi: 300
    code-link: true
    code-line-numbers: false
    code-copy: true
    code-fold: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-summary: Show code
    embed-resources: true
    number-sections: true
    auto-play-media: true
    highlight-style: kate
    width: 1200
    incremental: false
    reference-location: document
    transition: none
    logo: https://ig.conae.unc.edu.ar/wp-content/uploads/sites/68/2022/04/G-CLARO-C.png
    footer: |
      <rosa>**Víctor Gauto** <span class="line-md--cog-filled-loop"></span> Better science communication with <blanco>shiny</blanco> apps
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    hash-type: title
    # date: 2026-04-01
    center-title-slide: false
    navigation-mode: linear
    link-external-icon: false
    link-external-newwindow: true
    output-location: column
    theme:
      - extras/mi_estilo.scss
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel='shortcut icon' href='img/favicon.jpg'/>
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
  warning: false
lightbox: true
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
filters: 
  - roughnotation
---

# Outline {.center .unnumbered}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column  width="40%"}

- Introduction [$\S$ @sec-introduction]
- Motivation [$\S$ @sec-motivation]

:::

::: {.column  width="60%"}

<center>Slides available online</center>

::: {.r-fit-text}

```{.r}
https://vhgauto.github.io/seminario4-gulich-slides/
```

![](img/qr_seminario.svg){width="70%" fig-align="center"}

:::

:::

::::

# Introduction {#sec-introduction .center}

- Apps are everywhere, but not always associated with [data science]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3 fragment-index=1} communication.
- Using apps, the user is compelled to [explore]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3 fragment-index=2} and [play]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3 fragment-index=2} with the available options, changing the outputs at will.
- A good app can trigger [curiosity]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3 fragment-index=3} and allows our audience to ask new questions.

<center>

{{< iconify fluent:apps-48-filled size=5x style="color:#F79CD4;" >}}

</center>

# Motivation {#sec-motivation .center}

Once a **Research Project** is finished, we proceed to [communicate]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} the primary results.

- <u>Academic environment</u>: papers, congress, posters, conferences.
- Web site, social network, news outlet.

But, what are our tools to [engage]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} with the audience?

- Short, impactful titles.
- Good visualizations.
- A proper narrative to explain complex ideas.

An alternative, we can change the audience approach: from a [passive]{.rn-fragment rn-type=crossed-off rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} perspective, to an [active]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} and more engaging perspective.

# Reactive programming

<!-- {.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} -->

To click/tap a button and the result to appear as we ask for it is an interesting way to communicate [science]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}.

Since we work with data, we are used to code in a [direct way]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}, when the functions are evaluated immediately and an output is generated at the spot.

But, in a **reactive environment**, we have to think in a [potential user]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} and the many request that we can do, from the offer that we provide.

The app is divided in two main parts: the [user interface]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}, in the audience side, with the possibility to request calculations and the ability to configure settings. And the processing side, the [server]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}, where all the processes are executed accordingly.

## Challenges

Since the app is made of an user interface and a server:

- We must think in all the [potential stages]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} of variables and how the functions are associated within each other. 

- The [execution time]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} is a major aspect in the whole app, to offer a seamless user experience.

- [Reproducibility]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} is a main concern, to ensure good results.

# Why <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada>? {.center}

- <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> is a tool for app development, with [`r`]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} and [`python`]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} integration.
- Since a lot of work is done by [coding processing]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=2}, <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> offers a known framework.
- With [minimal configuration]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}, a proper app is achieved.
- Multiple [publishing alternatives]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=4} are available, some of them for free.
- <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> allows to style the app as we like, since we can include web develompent tools.
- [Resources]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=5} available online and package documentation with books and plenty of examples.

# When to develop a <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> app? {#sec-static .center}

Common contexts were one can rely on <quarto-portada>shiny</quarto-portada> to develop an app.

## Data exploration

A new dataset require data exploration, that usually initiate with statistical calculations, relationships between variables and plotting.

In a traditional way, this involves a lot of iteration, to check lots of potentially uninteresting results and modify observations ranges.

## Science communication

At the time to communicate our results, an app can engage our audience.

It's important to include proper explanations that follow along the reactive aspects of the app. Even color palette selection, or the capability to export some results is a desired app feature.

To select the variables to plot can improve the understanding of the insight found.

Since a web app is still a web site, we can include links, videos, plots and other resources to increase the user engagement.

## Remote sensing example

> - Interactive maps can be displayed by selecting different algorithms/parameters or RGB.
> - Spectral signature plots, for atmospheric correction comparisons.
> - Date selection for map inspection.
> - Color palette settings for a personal touch.
> - Export results as a .pdf report.
> - Display main parameter values, for an easy visualization, as a dashboard.
> - Include documentation, links, resources and formatted bibliography.

# Conclusion {#sec-conclusion .center}

> - Coding can be [scary]{.rn-fragment rn-type=crossed-off rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} [challenging]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} sometimes, but the results are remarkable.
> - The different maps formats that can be created (static, animated, interactive, 3D) [worth a try]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3}.
> - [Reproducibility]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} is important in data analysis but is equaly important in maps plotting.
> - This seminar it's a glimpse of mapping with code using <rosa>R</rosa>, and a [starting point]{.rn-fragment rn-type=box rn-color=#F79CD4 rn-strokeWidth=3} to start using the scripts available.

# Resources {#sec-resources .smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} ggplot2: Elegant Graphics for Data Analysis (3e)](https://ggplot2-book.org/maps.html)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} Making Maps with R](https://bookdown.org/nicohahn/making_maps_with_r5/docs/introduction.html)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} R as GIS for Economists](https://tmieno2.github.io/R-as-GIS-for-Economists-Quarto/chapters/08-CreateMaps-ggplot.html)

[{{< iconify ion:book-sharp >}} ggiraph-book](https://www.ardata.fr/ggiraph-book/)

---

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} Mapping wildfires data in R with mapsf](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_BN7xOtbVAc)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} Making maps in R using TMAP](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QcR9s3wF5lY)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} How to FULLY Customize Leaflet Maps using R programming](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U0aJeaCnMeE)

[{{< iconify mdi:youtube >}} 3D digital elevation maps with elevatr, rayshader and rayvista in R](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kGadI6_ZIR4)

::: {.callout style='font-size: 2em;'}
Presentation made with {{< quarto >}} & {{< iconify fa7-brands:r-project style="color:#74aadb;" >}}

[Code available at {{< iconify mdi:github >}}](https://github.com/vhgauto/seminario3-gulich)
:::

:::

::: {.column}

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} mapgl, R interface to Mapbox GL JS v3 and Maplibre GL JS](https://walker-data.com/mapgl/)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} elevatr, An R package for accessing elevation data](https://github.com/USEPA/elevatr)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} marmap, Import, Plot and Analyze Bathymetric and Topographic Data](https://github.com/ericpante/marmap)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} maptiles, Download, compose and display map tiles with R](https://github.com/riatelab/maptiles)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} geodata, download geographic data](https://rspatial.github.io/geodata/)

[{{< iconify ph:package-fill >}} rayvista, An R plugin for {rayshader} to view a 3D vista anywhere on earth](https://github.com/h-a-graham/rayvista)

---

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} MapLibre GL JS](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-gl-js/docs/)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} Mapbox GL JS](https://www.mapbox.com/)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} State of the (open source) web map](https://tim-salabim.codeberg.page/sotwm/sotwm.html)

[{{< iconify fluent-emoji-high-contrast:link >}} Tutorial: Adding Open Street Map Data to Rayshader Maps in R](https://www.tylermw.com/posts/data_visualization/adding-open-street-map-data-to-rayshader-maps-in-r.html)

:::

::::

# {.unnumbered background-color="#f79cd4"}

::: {style="text-align: center;"}

<gracias>Thanks for your time</gracias>

:::

<br>

:::: {.columns layout-ncol=2}

::: {.column style="text-align: center; font-size: 1em;"}

**MS Víctor Gauto**<br>[`victor.gauto@ca.frre.utn.edu.ar`](mailto:victor.gauto@ca.frre.utn.edu.ar)

:::

::: {.column layout-nrow=1 style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em;"}

[<blanco>{{< iconify ic:baseline-home >}}</blanco>](https://vhgauto.github.io/)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:twitter >}}</blanco>](https://twitter.com/vhgauto)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:instagram >}}</blanco>](https://www.instagram.com/vhgauto/)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:github >}}</blanco>](https://github.com/vhgauto)

[<blanco>{{< iconify ri:bluesky-fill >}}</blanco>](https://bsky.app/profile/vhgauto.bsky.social)

[<blanco>{{< iconify mdi:mastodon >}}</blanco>](https://fosstodon.org/@vhgauto)

`@vhgauto`

:::

::::

::: {layout-nrow=1 layout-valign="center"}

<center>

![](img/Gistaq_sinfondo.png){width=165}

![](img/logo_ig3.png){width=165}

![](img/iidthh.png){width=165}

![](img/Conicet_Logo_con_letras.png){width=165}

</center>

:::

::: {layout-nrow=1 layout-valign="center"}

<center>

![](img/logo_utn6.png){width=165}

![](img/unne.png){width=165}

![](img/UNC_03_NEGRO.svg){width=165}

![](img/CONAE.png){width=165}

</center>

:::

<center><span style='font-size: 7pt;'>*No generative AI was used to make this presentation*</span></center>
